title: The Lost Island Quest
summary: Sam and Maya find a mysterious old paper at school library.
coverScene: Sam and Maya stand on a bright beach looking at a strange map under a blue sky

sentences.text: Sam and Maya be at the school when they find a strange map in the library.
The map show a new island that be far from any place that people know on the sea.
They plan a trip and travel by boat to visit the location.
The boat be old but it can hold both people and enough food for a week of travel.
Sam and Maya feel happy because they hope the island will bring a great present for the people.
The map be old but the line be still clear, and the lines appear to light in the dark.
The map be draw by an old man who love the sea.
They leave the school early in the morning and row across the sea to the new island.
The sky be clear and the wind be calm, so the boat move fast to the place.
When they arrive they see a small beach and a tall tree that show the spot on the map.
They set the boat down and walk to the tree, where they find a stone box.
Inside the box is a note that say the gift will be a present for the people if they can answer questions.
The boat move soft over the water, and the team feel calm as the sea reach far.
The water be calm and the fish swim near the boat.
Sam and Maya talk about the map and the hope they have.
The sun light bright over the water as they sail.
The first question ask them to find the number of steps between the tree and the sea.
Sam count the steps and see that there be many steps, which be the right answer.
Maya write the answer on the box and the box open with a soft sound.
Inside they find a small wood key and another map that show a new room near the sea.
The key be bright and it appear to light the area when they hold it near the sun.
After they answer the first question, a soft wind guide them toward the far part of the island.
The note inside be write in a simple hand and it promise a gift.
They feel happy and calm as they read the note.
They use the key to open a stone gate that lead to a new room full of sound.
Inside the room they hear a soft voice that ask them to answer a second question.
The voice say the answer be the name of the sea that touch the island.
Sam think about the map and remember that the sea be called the blue sea.
Maya say the answer be blue sea, and the voice laugh with happy.
When the voice hear their answer, the stone gate move and a bright path appear on the place.
The voice be good and it guide them with soft light.
The room be still and the air feel cool.
The stone gate open wide and a bright light reveal a chest made of wood and stone.
Inside the chest they find a large bag of seed and a note that say the gift will help the people grow.
The note explain that the seed be from a rare plant that can give food for many years.
Sam and Maya take the bag of seed and the note and set the boat for home.
The boat arrive at the town place as the sun start to set, and the people welcome them.
The bag of seed be heavy but the box be strong, and they can carry it without trouble.
The seed be small but each hold a promise of future food.
The people in the town plan to plant the seed in the best area.
The bag of seed be kept in a safe place until the planting season.
The people gather around and listen as Sam and Maya tell the story of the island and the gift.
They give the seed to the farmers and the farmers plant them in the field across the town.
Soon the field grow green and the town have enough food for the whole year.
The present from the island make the town strong and happy, and Sam and Maya feel good.
If they had not find the map, the town would have miss the gift and would have struggle.
During the celebration, the old people thank Sam and Maya for their strong look and share stories of past trips.
The old people tell stories of past trips and share knowledge with the youth.
The youth listen and learn about respect for nature and the sea.
The town celebrate with music and dance as the new crop grow.
Now they know that a simple map can change the life of many people in their community.
They hope that other children will look for new place and will help their own town.
The island will remain a secret but the gift will stay in the heart of the town.
Sam and Maya will always remember the trip and the map that lead them to the great present.
Their story show that interest, team, and wait can bring good results for everyone.
It also remind us that simple tools like a map can open path to new world.
They decide to share the map with other school to inspire new task.
Each child who read the story feel a fire of interest.
The story travel beyond the town and inspire other area.
Sam and Maya choose to write a report about their look for the school class.
They describe the map, the boat, the island, and the questions they answer.
The teacher thank their work and say the story could encourage many other students.
The class choose to create a club that would look at near maps and share new ideas.
Soon the club meet each week and many children join, hope to find new place like Sam and Maya.
The club meet in the library and each member bring a new map to discuss.
They plan a future trip to visit a new place together.
Their story show that interest, team, and wait can lead to great results for a whole community.
The club plan to hold a year show of maps and stories.

quiz.q: What did Sam and Maya find in the library?
How did Sam and Maya travel to the island?
What did the island gift to the village?

sentences.text: Sam and Maya are at the school.

They plan a trip and decide to travel by boat.
The boat is old but it can hold both of them.
It also can hold food for a week.

Inside they find a small wood key.

After they solve the first puzzle a soft wind guide them.

When the voice hear their answer the stone gate move.

The stone gate open.
A bright light reveal a chest made of wood and stone.
Inside the chest they find a large bag of seed.
They also find a note that say the gift will help the village grow.
The note explain that the seed are from a rare plant.
The plant can give food for many years.
Sam and Maya take the bag of seed and the note.
They set the boat for home.
The boat come at the village shore as the sun begin to set.
The villagers meet them.
The bag of seed is heavy but the box is strong.
They can carry it without trouble.
The villagers meet and listen.
Sam and Maya tell the story of the island and the gift.
They give the seed to the people.
The people put the seed in the ground.
Soon the ground grow green.
The village have enough food for the whole year.
The gift from the island make the village strong and happy.
Sam and Maya feel good.
If they had not found the map the village would miss the gift.
It would struggle.
During the celebration the old thank Sam and Maya for their strong look.
They share story of past trips.
Now they know that a simple map can change the life of many people.
They hope that people will look for new places.
They will help their own villages.
The island will remain a secret.
The gift will stay in the heart of the village.
Sam and Maya decide to write a report about their look.
The report is for the school class.
They describe the map, the boat, the island and the questions they solved.
The teacher say their work is good.
The story could encourage many other students.
The class decide to create a club.
The club will look at near maps and share new plan.
Soon the club meet each week.
Many people join and hope to find new places like Sam and Maya.
The story show that interest, team and wait can lead to great results.
It helps a whole community.
It also show us that simple tools like a map can open paths.
It can lead to new worlds.
